channel.title: NPR Topics: Economy
channel.description: NPR news on the U.S. and world economy, the World Bank, and Federal Reserve. Commentary on economic trends. Subscribe to NPR Economy podcasts and RSS feeds.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: They graduate to six figure salaries, and grueling work
item.description: Cadets from the nation's Merchant Marine academies are finding lots of demand and great salaries because of a shortage of licensed mariners.
item.content:encoded:
Cadets from the nation's Merchant Marine academies are finding lots of demand and great salaries because of a shortage of licensed mariners.

(Image credit: SUNY Maritime College)
item.dc:creator: Steve Kastenbaum

item.title: Prices are up, but Mother's Day still means brunch
item.description: It's the biggest day for America's restaurants, and one of the biggest for flower sales. Despite anxieties over rising gas prices, people are ready to splurge to celebrate.
item.content:encoded:
It's the biggest day for America's restaurants, and one of the biggest for flower sales. Despite anxieties over rising gas prices, people are ready to splurge to celebrate.

(Image credit: Brandon Bell)
item.dc:creator: Alina Selyukh

item.title: Democrats want to raise taxes on the wealthy. A look at the math
item.description: Democrats want to tax the rich to cut taxes on worker incomes. Democratic Sen. Chris Van Hollen of Maryland explains and a tax policy expert shares his reservations with NPR's Planet Money team.
item.content:encoded: Democrats want to tax the rich to cut taxes on worker incomes. Democratic Sen. Chris Van Hollen of Maryland explains and a tax policy expert shares his reservations with NPR's Planet Money team.
item.dc:creator: Darian Woods

item.title: Trade court strikes down a second round of Trump tariffs
item.description: The Court of International Trade has struck down a second round of global tariffs ordered by President Trump, after his earlier import taxes were outlawed by the U.S. Supreme Court.
item.content:encoded:
The Court of International Trade has struck down a second round of global tariffs ordered by President Trump, after his earlier import taxes were outlawed by the U.S. Supreme Court.

(Image credit: Joe Raedle)
item.dc:creator: Scott Horsley

item.title: She helps families in need. As gas and grocery prices rise, she needs help, too
item.description: She has spent decades helping others struggling to make ends meet. Now the rising cost of gas and groceries has left Dalene Basden feeling the strain herself.
item.content:encoded:
She has spent decades helping others struggling to make ends meet. Now the rising cost of gas and groceries has left Dalene Basden feeling the strain herself.

(Image credit: Tovia Smith/NPR)
item.dc:creator: Tovia Smith

item.title: Poll: Most Americans say Trump deserves blame for high gas prices
item.description: The new NPR/PBS News/Marist poll finds that 8 in 10 Americans say gas prices are straining their household budgets.
item.content:encoded: The new NPR/PBS News/Marist poll finds that 8 in 10 Americans say gas prices are straining their household budgets.
item.dc:creator: Domenico Montanaro

item.title: A trip to Europe? In this economy? Expensive flights keep vacations closer to home
item.description: Expensive airfares are causing Americans to change their travel plans. Travel advisors are seeing a drop in bookings and warn high prices are here to stay.
item.content:encoded: Expensive airfares are causing Americans to change their travel plans. Travel advisors are seeing a drop in bookings and warn high prices are here to stay.
item.dc:creator: Stephan Bisaha

item.title: Have you lost or are worried about losing SNAP benefits? NPR wants to hear your story
item.description: NPR wants to hear from people impacted by changes to the Supplemental Nutrition Assistance Program.
item.content:encoded:
NPR wants to hear from people impacted by changes to the Supplemental Nutrition Assistance Program.

(Image credit: Joe Raedle)
item.dc:creator: NPR Staff

item.title: How much is the war hitting American's bottom line?
item.description: NPR's Mary Louise Kelly speaks with Martha Gimbel, executive director and co-founder of the Budget Lab at Yale, about living in a war economy.
item.content:encoded: NPR's Mary Louise Kelly speaks with Martha Gimbel, executive director and co-founder of the Budget Lab at Yale, about living in a war economy.
item.dc:creator: Karen Zamora

item.title: If you are struggling to make ends meet, NPR wants to hear from you
item.description: With gas prices and other necessities at record highs, families are struggling with costs. NPR wants to know how you're coping.
item.content:encoded:
With gas prices and other necessities at record highs, families are struggling with costs. NPR wants to know how you're coping.

(Image credit: Jenny Kane)
item.dc:creator: NPR Staff
